2603.20976.title: Detection of adversarial intent in Human-AI teams using LLMs
2603.20976.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in human-AI teams as support agents for complex tasks such as information retrieval, programming, and decision-making assistance. While these agents' autonomy and contextual knowledge enables them to be useful, it also exposes them to a broad range of attacks, including data poisoning, prompt injection, and even prompt engineering. Through these attack vectors, malicious actors can manipulate an LLM agent to provide harmful information, potentially manipulating human agents to make harmful decisions. While prior work has focused on LLMs as attack targets or adversarial actors, this paper studies their potential role as defensive supervisors within mixed human-AI teams. Using a dataset consisting of multi-party conversations and decisions for a real human-AI team over a 25 round horizon, we formulate the problem of malicious behavior detection from interaction traces. We find that LLMs are capable of identifying malicious behavior in real-time, and without task-specific information, indicating the potential for task-agnostic defense. Moreover, we find that the malicious behavior of interest is not easily identified using simple heuristics, further suggesting the introduction of LLM defenders could render human teams more robust to certain classes of attack.
2603.20976.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地被部署在人類與人工智慧的團隊中，作為複雜任務的支援代理，例如信息檢索、程式設計和決策輔助。雖然這些代理的自主性和上下文知識使它們變得有用，但也使它們暴露於廣泛的攻擊範圍，包括數據中毒、提示注入，甚至提示工程。透過這些攻擊途徑，惡意行為者可以操縱LLM代理提供有害信息，並可能操縱人類代理做出有害決策。雖然先前的研究集中於LLMs作為攻擊目標或對抗行為者，但本論文研究它們在混合人類-人工智慧團隊中作為防禦監督者的潛在角色。使用一個包含多方對話和決策的數據集，針對一個真實的人類-人工智慧團隊進行25輪的研究，我們將惡意行為檢測問題從互動痕跡中進行了形式化。我們發現LLMs能夠實時識別惡意行為，且不需要特定任務的信息，這表明了任務無關的防禦潛力。此外，我們發現感興趣的惡意行為並不容易通過簡單的啟發式方法識別，進一步表明引入LLM防禦者可能使人類團隊對某些類別的攻擊更具韌性。
2603.20976.author: Abed K. Musaffar et.al.
2603.20976.authors: Abed K. Musaffar, Ambuj Singh, Francesco Bullo
